title: Kawasaki Signs Deal to Build World's Largest Liquefied Hydrogen Carrier
content: Kawasaki Heavy Industries and Japan Suiso Energy (JSE) have signed a contract to construct the worldâ€™s largest liquefied hydrogen carrier, featuring a 40,000 mÂ³ capacity. Scheduled for construction at Kawasakiâ€™s Sakaide Works, the vessel is a core component of a NEDO Green Innovation Fund Project aimed at demonstrating a commercial-scale hydrogen supply chain by FY2030. Building on the success of the 1,250 mÂ³ SUISO FRONTIER, this new carrier is designed with a high-performance insulation system to minimize boil-off gas (BOG). It features a hydrogen/oil dual-fuel propulsion system that utilizes BOG as fuel to reduce COâ‚‚ emissions during transport. JSE will operate the vessel to validate the safety, reliability, and economics of global hydrogen transport, utilizing the Kawasaki LH2 Terminal currently under construction. This project represents a significant stride toward a carbon-neutral society by enabling stable, large-volume hydrogen supply.
author: Analysts Account
